2603.20007.title: Physics-Informed Long-Range Coulomb Correction for Machine-learning Hamiltonians
2603.20007.paper_summary: Machine-learning electronic Hamiltonians achieve orders-of-magnitude speedups over density-functional theory, yet current models omit long-range Coulomb interactions that govern physics in polar crystals and heterostructures. We derive closed-form long-range Hamiltonian matrix elements in a nonorthogonal atomic-orbital basis through variational decomposition of the electrostatic energy, deriving a variationally consistent mapping from the electron density matrix to effective atomic charges. We implement this framework in HamGNN-LR, a dual-channel architecture combining E(3)-equivariant message passing with reciprocal-space Ewald summation. Benchmarks demonstrate that physics-based long-range corrections are essential: purely data-driven attention mechanisms fail to capture macroscopic electrostatic potentials. Benchmarks on polar ZnO slabs, CdSe/ZnS heterostructures, and GaN/AlN superlattices show two- to threefold error reductions and robust transferability to systems far beyond training sizes, eliminating the characteristic staircase artifacts that plague short-range models in the presence of built-in electric fields.
2603.20007.paper_summary_zh: 機器學習電子哈密頓量在計算速度上比密度泛函理論快了幾個數量級，但目前的模型忽略了在極性晶體和異質結構中主導物理的長程庫倫相互作用。我們通過對靜電能量的變分分解，推導出非正交原子軌道基底中的閉式長程哈密頓量矩陣元素，並從電子密度矩陣導出變分一致的有效原子電荷映射。我們在HamGNN-LR中實現了這一框架，這是一種雙通道架構，結合了E(3)-等變消息傳遞和倒數空間Ewald求和。基準測試顯示，基於物理的長程修正是必不可少的：純粹數據驅動的注意機制無法捕捉宏觀靜電勢。對極性ZnO薄片、CdSe/ZnS異質結構和GaN/AlN超晶格的基準測試顯示，誤差減少了兩到三倍，並且對超出訓練規模的系統具有穩健的可轉移性，消除了在內建電場存在下短程模型所困擾的階梯狀伪影。
2603.20007.author: Yang Zhong et.al.
2603.20007.authors: Yang Zhong, Xiwen Li, Xingao Gong, Hongjun Xiang
